PluginManagerPlugin.title: Plugin Manager
PluginManagerPlugin.helpBody: Enable or disable built-in plugins and review installed third-party plugins.

browse.empty: No plugins are listed in the registry yet.
browse.error: Could not load the plugin registry.
browse.installed: Installed. Rebuilding — the app will reload shortly.
browse.installFailed: Install failed. See the terminal for details.

develop.intro: KeepTrack is built from dozens of standalone plugins, and you can write your own. A plugin is a small package that adds a drawer icon, menu, overlay, or tool. Plugins are developed and installed with the plugin CLI from a KeepTrack checkout.
develop.create: Scaffold a new plugin (interactive: it asks for a name, kind, and description).
develop.preview: Boot the app with only your plugin enabled, with live reload as you edit.
develop.rule1: Your entry file must export a class matching className in keeptrack-plugin.json.
develop.rule2: That class's readonly id must equal its configKey.

labels.externalEmpty: No external plugins installed. Add one with the plugin CLI.
labels.installHint: Installing a plugin requires rebuilding KeepTrack. Copy this command and run it in your KeepTrack checkout.

help.overview: The Plugin Manager lets you turn KeepTrack's built-in plugins on or off and see which third-party plugins are installed. Because plugins load when the app starts, changes take effect after a reload.
help.toggles: Flip a switch to enable or disable a plugin, then click Reload to apply. Always-on infrastructure plugins are locked and cannot be disabled.
help.external: Third-party plugins installed via the plugin CLI appear in their own section with their version, a compatibility badge, and a link to their source. To install a new one, copy the command from the Install section and run it in your KeepTrack checkout.
